name: Skill Library implementation map
description: Skill Library logic lives primarily in extensions/multi-agent/skill-discovery.ts, server endpoints in extensions/multi-agent/server.ts, Pi-facing skill tools in extensions/multi-agent/index.ts, and UI in web/app.tsx. Skill discovery delegates to Pi's DefaultResourceLoader, uses content-hash guards for edits, classifies project/global skills as editable, and supports directory skill trees plus safe text/markdown file preview/editing.

title: Skill library is constrained to discovered skills and editable roots
rationale: Dashboard/tool endpoints should not become arbitrary file read/write APIs. Skill reads resolve IDs only against discovered skills, file reads stay within a skill base directory, and writes require editable roots such as project .pi/skills or global Pi agent skills. Package skills remain preview/read-only by default.

title: Orchestrator Library skill refs resolve to explicit spawn paths
rationale: Skill templates should store namespaced Orchestrator Library refs such as `team:skills/example-analysis/SKILL.md` instead of bare skill names when possible. Capability resolution converts those refs into concrete SKILL.md paths and spawn guards reject duplicate runtime skill names before launching child agents, allowing `spawnAgent` to pass exact repeatable `--skill` paths without adding library skills to generic Pi settings.

title: Copy Skill derives discovered skills into editable Pi skill scopes with unique names
rationale: The Skill Library copy flow copies only already-discovered skill directories/files into project or global .pi/agent skill scopes, rewrites SKILL.md frontmatter with a required new name/description, and rejects duplicate discovered names because Pi skill-name collisions keep the first discovered skill.

name: Skill Library detail actions require selected-detail identity match
description: When a dashboard list selection fetches detail asynchronously, clear stale detail/tree/file state immediately on selected id change and only render edit/delete/save/file controls when loaded detail.skill.id matches the current selected skill id. This prevents transient destructive actions from targeting the previously selected skill.

title: New skills default to the first configured Orchestrator Library
rationale: Skill creation now targets an explicitly selected Orchestrator Library when provided, otherwise defaults to the first valid configured library in load order. When no library is configured and no legacy scope is explicitly requested, creation falls back to the global Pi skills folder; dashboard UI exposes library targets plus global fallback, not executable extension authoring.

title: Package-provided skills carry explicit dashboard metadata
rationale: Skill discovery now marks skills as packageProvided when their source metadata or canonical path indicates package-manager installation paths such as ~/.pi/agent/npm/node_modules, ~/.bun/install/global/node_modules, or node_modules. The Skill Library renders a separate 'package' badge while preserving existing global/project/library scope labels and editability semantics.
